summary:
Only two structures fuse to create the upper lip—the **maxillary processes** and the **medial nasal processes**, which together form its central and lateral portions respectively. This understanding highlights that incorrect options may include unrelated elements such as the lateral nasal processes or other facial components involved in different anatomical regions like nose development rather than lip morphogenesis.
